name: tailwind-css-v4-mastery
description: Expert guidance for leveraging Tailwind CSS V4's new Oxide engine, CSS-first configuration, and modern styling paradigms. This skill transforms Claude into a Tailwind V4 architecture specialist, capable of designing component systems, optimizing performance, and executing complex styling challenges with precision.
capabilities: Migrate projects from Tailwind v3 to v4 with automated scripts
Design component systems using CSS-first @theme configuration
Optimize build performance with Oxide engine (10-100x faster)
Create multi-theme and enterprise brand systems
Generate CSS variables and modern styling patterns
author: Calel33
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing Tailwind CSS V4 guides and one benign migration shell script. The static analyzer generated 435 false positives by misinterpreting markdown backticks as shell execution, documentation URLs as network threats, and text patterns like 'md' as weak cryptography. All findings are false positives - no cryptographic code, no credential access, no exfiltration patterns, no malicious behavior exists. The migration script only performs standard file backup and sed operations for CSS migration purposes.
valueStatement: Tailwind CSS V4 introduces revolutionary changes with its Oxide engine and CSS-first configuration. This skill provides expert guidance to navigate migration, optimize performance, and build modern design systems.
userTitle: Master Tailwind CSS V4 with AI-powered guidance
limitations: No support for Tailwind v3 or legacy configuration files
Focused on CSS architecture, not framework-specific implementations
Migration script handles common patterns but may need manual review

useCases.title: Migrate from Tailwind v3 to v4
useCases.description: Automated migration with breaking change detection, utility updates, and configuration conversion from JavaScript to CSS-first approach.

useCases.targetUser: Design system architects and UI engineers
useCases.title: Build modern component libraries
useCases.description: Create scalable design systems with CSS variables, multi-theme support, and component-scoped styling using V4's architecture.

useCases.title: Optimize build performance
useCases.description: Achieve 10-100x faster builds with Oxide engine configuration, plugin selection, and CSS optimization patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Migration Check
promptTemplates.scenario: I have a v3 project - what breaks?
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to migrate my Tailwind v3 project to v4. Can you check what breaking changes affect my current setup? I use shadows, rounded corners, and opacity utilities extensively.

promptTemplates.title: Component System Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me design a button component system in Tailwind V4 with primary/secondary variants, sizes, and dark mode support using @theme configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Performance Optimization
promptTemplates.scenario: My builds are slow with PostCSS
promptTemplates.prompt: My Tailwind v4 builds are taking 2-3 seconds with PostCSS. How can I optimize this? I'm using Webpack in a Next.js project.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Brand Enterprise Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to support 5 different brand themes in our enterprise app. Show me how to set up CSS variables and theme switching with data attributes in Tailwind V4.

bestPractices: Always use -- prefix in @theme blocks: --color-primary not color-primary
Choose the right plugin for your build tool: @tailwindcss/vite for Vite, @tailwindcss/postcss for Webpack
Test border colors and ring widths after migration - defaults changed significantly
antiPatterns: Don't keep tailwind.config.js - V4 ignores it completely. Use @theme in CSS instead
Avoid defining complete color palettes - override only what you need from defaults
Never use opacity utilities - they're removed. Use color modifiers like bg-black/50 instead

faq.question: Is this compatible with Tailwind v3 projects?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses exclusively on Tailwind CSS V4. V3 uses different configuration and utility names.

faq.question: What browsers does V4 support?
faq.answer: Modern browsers only: Safari 16.4+, Chrome 111+, Firefox 128+. Legacy support requires staying on v3.

faq.question: Can I use this with any framework?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with React, Vue, Svelte, or vanilla HTML. Plugin selection varies by build tool.

faq.question: Is my data safe during migration?
faq.answer: Yes, the migration script creates backups and only modifies CSS/HTML files, not your application logic.

faq.question: What if the migration script breaks something?
faq.answer: The script backs up all files to .tailwind-v3-backup/. You can manually review changes or restore originals.

faq.question: How does this compare to official Tailwind docs?
faq.answer: This skill provides decision trees, automated scripts, and practical patterns beyond basic documentation.
